title: 7 contracts Bruins might try to unload this offseason
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins have over $16 million in cap space, potentially increasing by shedding redundant contracts.
Joonas Korpisalo's $3 million contract is likely to be moved to free cap space for Bruins.
Elias Lindholm's $7.75 million contract, plagued by injuries, poses a challenge for Boston to offload.

title: First white shark sighting of 2026 confirmed off Massachusetts coast
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A dead gray seal with suspected shark bite was found on Martha's Vineyard, confirming the first white shark sighting of 2026.
John Chisholm, an Aquarium scientist, verified the seal's wounds as consistent with a white shark bite on May 11, 2026.
The Atlantic White Shark Conservancy's Sharktivity app helps the public report shark sightings and assists scientific research.

title: 10 stats to help contextualize the Red Sox’ rocky start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Red Sox are 0-21 when trailing after seven innings, highlighting their struggle with late-game comebacks.
Boston's lineup ranks 29th in MLB with just 29 home runs, lacking a significant power hitter this season.
Defensively, the team leads MLB with 41 defensive runs saved, showcasing strength despite offensive struggles.

title: Want to own a piece of Dunkin’? Its parent company may go public.
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Inspire Brands plans to go public, marking Dunkin's potential third appearance on the stock market in eight decades.
The Atlanta-based company has filed confidentially with the SEC for an IPO, although no specifics, such as date or price, are set.
Inspire Brands aims to use IPO proceeds to repay debt and cover offering expenses, following their $11.3 billion Dunkin' acquisition.

title: Trooper who helped take down Memorial Drive gunman identified as victims’ families speak out
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Trooper Landon Veney and an armed Marine veteran helped stop gunman Tyler Brown on Memorial Drive in Cambridge.
Tyler Brown, 46, faces multiple charges including armed assault with intent to murder following the chaotic shooting.
Two men, including a paratransit service driver and a DoorDash driver, were seriously injured in the attack.

title: A new grocery store is coming to the Seaport
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Whole Foods Market will open a smaller-format store, Daily Shop, at 111 Harbor Way in Boston's Seaport.
The Seaport store will offer a curated selection of produce, meat, seafood, prepared foods, and grab-and-go options.
Whole Foods plans additional Daily Shop locations in Chicago, Philadelphia, London, and New York City beyond the Seaport opening.

title: Travelers can now get real-time security checkpoint wait times at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Logan Airport introduces real-time security checkpoint wait times via a website and FlyLogan app.
Massport developed the tool using cameras to monitor line length and speed without recording personal details.
Logan Airport maintains its No. 10 rank in the J.D. Power 2025 Airport Satisfaction Study with improved scores.

title: MBTA video shows man throwing ‘temper tantrum’ and blocking traffic after missing bus
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A man blocked an MBTA bus on Mt. Auburn Street in Cambridge after missing it, causing traffic disruption.
The man faced charges along with a passenger who allegedly threatened him with a knife, police reported.
The passenger, found with active warrants, was arrested following the incident on May 6, transit police stated.

title: Mansfield becomes first town in Mass. to pass near-total ban on data centers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Mansfield approved a zoning amendment limiting data center operations to specific industrial zones with special permits for Tier I centers.
The bylaw caps data center energy usage at two megawatts, prohibiting larger facilities to protect local infrastructure resources.
Mansfield's new regulations include detailed compliance measures for noise and environmental impacts evaluated by town departments.

title: Spate of Boston gun violence continues with Dorchester shooting Tuesday
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A Dorchester shooting early Tuesday left one person in critical condition near 25 Dacia St., Boston police report.
This marks the fifth shooting incident in Boston in a week, with three occurring in Dorchester.
No arrests have been made in connection to the recent Boston shootings; the city's total shooting victims have decreased this year.
